codeReview.summary: Deployment and seeding scripts were added, and root dev startup was wired, but the integration workflow does not implement the required on-chain end-to-end flow (deposit/place/match/withdraw) or frontend state verification. The feature therefore does not fully satisfy its expectedBehavior.

issues.description: The integration test claims full on-chain coverage when APTOS_PRIVATE_KEY/CONTRACT_ADDRESS are provided, but the script never performs SDK-driven deposit/order/match/withdraw operations. It only runs service smoke checks, so required end-to-end behavior is not implemented.
Frontend validation is limited to HTTP 200 from the web root; there is no verification that seeded depth/trades appear in the UI, which is explicitly required by the feature expectedBehavior.
The test re-implements generatePriceLevels instead of importing the production function from seed-orderbook.ts, so regressions in the real script logic may not be detected.
Rate-limit verification soft-passes when no 429 responses are observed, which can produce false positives for an integration check intended to validate rate limiting behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill mandates strict tests-first (TDD), but this feature's transcript shows scripts created first and tests added later; this is recurring for deployment/ops scripts and suggests the skill procedure should explicitly allow script-first workflows with utility-level tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session 85edea34-5089-4c34-b4b2-6081ec079f6e shows Create on scripts/deploy-mainnet.sh, register-market.ts, seed-orderbook.ts, integration-test.ts before seed-orderbook.test.ts; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges also calls this out.

sharedStateObservations.observation: WebSocket healthcheck in services.yaml is a placeholder fallback and does not truly verify WS readiness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:27 uses `curl -sf http://localhost:3101/health 2>/dev/null || echo "ws check"`, but the WS server is websocket-native and this command can report success without validating a WS handshake.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The discovered Aptos events-v1 deprecation risk from the handoff is not captured in shared library docs, so future workers may repeat the same investigation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff for deployment-integration lists deprecated indexer events API as a discovered issue; .factory/library/backend-event-ordering.md documents ordering concerns but not the events-v1 deprecation/migration note.

summary: Review failed: key deployment/seeding pieces exist, but the integration workflow does not execute or verify the required on-chain E2E flow and frontend trade/depth updates, leaving core feature expectations unmet.
